title: GENERAL LEGAL AID - 211info
text: LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON DOUGLAS COUNTY OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine civil legal matters, usually regarding housing, public benefits, or expungement of criminal records. Also offers criminal record expungement services for acquittals or dismised charges, eviction court cases, uncharged arrests more than 1 year old, and convictions more than 3 years old and marijuana charges on a case by case basis. Eligibility: Expungement: Individuals who have no criminal charges in the U.S. or foreign countries, no criminal convictions in the previous 3 years, no more than one conviction in the past 10 years, and are not currently on parole or probation. All other services: Must meet income guidelines. Prioritizes serving survivors of abuse or violent trauma and disabled individuals. Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm Walk in hours: Wednesday 10am-noon, Thursday 1pm-4pm (800) 520-5292 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (800) 520-5292 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in or call for more information. If phone line rings busy, individuals are advised to hang up and call back. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, American Sign Language (ASL), interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Douglas County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located on the corner of Kane and Cass, 2 blocks south of the post office Services: Comprehensive Family Law Services Criminal Record Expungement Assistance General Legal Aid Other Services Offered At This Location: LEGAL SERVICES - SENIOR LAW PROJECT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. No Mapping Information Available
